body.title: audit-design Check 10: what could be encapsulated here?
body.result: added Check 10 (Encapsulation surface) to audit-design SKILL.md after Check 9; updated output format to 10/10; [encapsulation] finding tag; all-nine → all-ten in hard rules and steps
body.description:
audit-design has 9 checks. Add Check 10: 'Encapsulation surface — does this design have any subsystem reaching across an interface it shouldn't?' Look for: (a) component A knowing B's internals when a service interface would suffice, (b) shared mutable state without a proxy, (c) caller selecting from a list when a dispatcher could hand it the right item. Fail (AMEND) when the design introduces new cross-cutting reach that could instead be a named service. Advisory note (not AMEND) when an existing reach is named and accepted. This check follows the DB proxy / inference proxy / queue-as-black-box pattern.

**Affected files:** ~/.claude/skills/audit-design/SKILL.md (add Check 10 section after Check 9)
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Check 10 text only; no changes to existing checks 1–9; no changes to output format (add encapsulation to findings tag list)
**Test plan:** no automated tests — skill doc edit; verify by reading SKILL.md and confirming Check 10 present with AMEND condition and example finding tag '[encapsulation]'
**Validation:** audit-design SKILL.md contains '### Check 10' section; next /decided that introduces new coupling triggers the check
**Cleanup:** n/a
**Observable via:** audit-design output includes '[encapsulation]' finding tag when triggered
**Validation:** (1) Read ~/.claude/skills/audit-design/SKILL.md and confirm ### Check 10 section present with AMEND condition and [encapsulation] finding tag. (2) In the next /decided that introduces a new cross-subsystem call, confirm audit-design output contains [encapsulation] finding — either AMEND or advisory note.
**Cleanup:** n/a — skill doc edit is idempotent
